text: 1. With increasing Al content, the phase constitution transitions sequentially from single-phase austenite (4Al steel) to austenitematrix duplex (6Al steel), then to ferrite-matrix duplex structures (8Al and 10Al steels). $\mathrm { D O } _ { 3 }$ ordered domains coexist within both the B2 ordered domains and the ferrite, exhibiting progressive size enlargement with higher Al concentrations.

2. The 8Al steel exhibits multi-stage strain hardening characteristics due to planar slip in ferrite. The dislocation structure in ferrite exhibits distinct planar slip, forming slip bands whose spacing decreases during straining, in accordance with the dynamic slip band refinement (DSBR) mechanism.

3. The ferritic dislocation structure in 10Al steel is predominantly characterized by localized long straight screw dislocations, which leads to plastic instability in 10Al steel, whereas in 8Al steel, it is characterized by a uniform distribution of long straight screw and edge dislocations. The high stress at the austenite-ferrite interface facilitates the activation of edge dislocations in ferrite.

4. The size of the shear zone is estimated based on the number of paired dislocations at slip band fronts. The estimated size in 8Al steel is less than 1.5 nm, which is very close to the TEM-observed value of less than 2.1 nm. The estimated size in 10Al steel is less than 3.0 nm, which is much smaller than the TEM-observed value of less than 19.0 nm.

5. It is found that as the Al content increases from 4 % to 8 %, the yield strength and tensile strength increase from 239 (±7) MPa and 552 (±12) MPa to 455 (±10) MPa and 74 (±14) MPa, respectively, while the uniform elongation only decreases from 38.5 (±2.0) % to 31.0 (±1.3) %. The synergistic operation of edge dislocation activity and DSBR effect in ferritic matrices underlies the superior strengthductility balance observed in the 8Al ferrite-based duplex lightweight steel. # CRediT authorship contribution statement Qingfeng Kang: Writing – original draft, Methodology, Formal analysis, Data curation, Conceptualization. Zexi Zhang: Resources, Investigation. Hui Wang: Resources, Methodology, Investigation. Haifeng Xu: Formal analysis, Data curation. Cunyu Wang: Methodology, Investigation, Funding acquisition. Zhengdong Liu: Resources, Funding acquisition. Wenquan Cao: Writing – review & editing, Supervision, Resources, Project administration, Funding acquisition. # Declaration of competing interest The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper. # Acknowledgement This research is supported by the Advanced Materials National Science and Technology Major Project (No. 2025ZD0611101) and Natural Nature Science Foundation of China (No. 51871062). # Data availability Data will be made available on request.
